summary: Jeff Atwood discusses a frustrating limitation in Visual Studio .NET's code outlining feature: XML comments and function bodies are outlined as separate blocks rather than together. This forces developers to wrap functions with their comments in regions to achieve the expected collapsing behavior. Despite hoping for improvement, VS.NET 2005 exhibits the same problem. Atwood mentions working on a macro solution and recommends existing Documentator macros as a workaround.
key_points: Visual Studio .NET outlines XML comments and function code as separate collapsible blocks, which is counterintuitive
Using regions around functions with their comments provides the unified outlining behavior developers actually want
The problem persists in VS.NET 2005, indicating Microsoft didn't prioritize this usability issue
Macros offer a workaround for automatically adding regions to functions
money_quotes: The default Visual Studio .NET outlining support leaves a lot to be desired.
It's aggravating that there is no built in support for outlining the entire function and the comments.
I just checked, and VS.NET 2005 (aka Whidbey) has this same bad behavior. What a bummer.
key_insight: When IDE features don't match developer expectations for code organization, workarounds like regions become necessary conventions despite adding ceremony to the codebase.
